tes_thermal.name: Thermal Energy Storage (TES)
tes_thermal.pros: Lowest $/kWh cost, proven technology, no critical materials
tes_thermal.cons: Low round-trip efficiency (33%), minimum load constraint

lithium_ion.name: Lithium-Ion Battery
lithium_ion.pros: High efficiency (88%), fast response, no minimum load
lithium_ion.cons: High $/kWh ($150), fire risk, supply chain constraints, degradation

flow_battery.name: Vanadium Flow Battery
flow_battery.pros: Long cycle life, independent power/energy scaling, no degradation
flow_battery.cons: High $/kWh ($180), complex maintenance, vanadium supply risk

hydrogen.name: Hydrogen Storage
hydrogen.pros: Lowest $/kWh storage, infinite duration, hydrogen economy synergy
hydrogen.cons: Low efficiency (38%), high conversion costs, safety concerns

ira_itc_30pct.name: IRA 30% Investment Tax Credit
ira_itc_30pct.description: Section 48E: 30% ITC on solar, wind, and energy storage
key_details: Applies to solar, wind, and standalone energy storage

carbon_pricing_50.name: Carbon Price: $50/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_50.description: Carbon tax or cap-and-trade at $50/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_50.impact_on_100cfe_competitiveness: Moderate - gas at $60 vs TES equiv $121-125

carbon_pricing_100.name: Carbon Price: $100/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_100.description: Carbon tax or cap-and-trade at $100/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_100.impact_on_100cfe_competitiveness: High - gas at $80 vs TES equiv $121-125

carbon_pricing_200.name: Carbon Price: $200/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_200.description: Carbon tax or cap-and-trade at $200/ton CO₂
carbon_pricing_200.impact_on_100cfe_competitiveness: Very High - gas at $120 vs TES equiv $121-125 (crossover)

interconnection_costs.name: Grid Interconnection Requirements
interconnection_costs.description: Costs to connect 400+ MW solar/wind to grid
key_details: Network upgrade costs vary by location ($50k-$200k/MW)
Queue times: 2-5 years in congested regions
Off-grid datacenter avoids interconnection costs entirely

local_emissions_regulations.name: Local Air Quality Regulations
local_emissions_regulations.description: NOx, SOx, PM2.5 emission limits for gas turbines
key_details: 100% CFE avoids all local emission compliance costs

renewable_energy_credits.name: Renewable Energy Credits (RECs)
renewable_energy_credits.description: Revenue from selling RECs for renewable generation
key_details: Google, Microsoft pay premium for 24/7 matching

short_term_target.title: Phase 1: Deploy 90% CFE System (2027-2028)
short_term_target.configuration: 400 MW solar + 75 MW wind + 150 MW TES (24 hr)
rationale: 90% CFE achieves dramatic decarbonization (90% reduction vs. gas baseline)
LCOE $80/MWh competitive with grid power in many regions
Remaining 10% can use biogas, hydrogen, or REC purchases
Proven technology, lower risk than 100% CFE stretch goal

long_term_target.title: Phase 2: Achieve 100% CFE (2030+)
long_term_target.configuration: 400 MW solar + 125 MW wind + 180 MW TES (44 hr)
rationale: Complete elimination of fossil fuel backup
LCOE $113/MWh economically viable with carbon pricing ($100+/ton CO₂)

technology_choice.title: Choose TES over Batteries for Long-Duration Storage
rationale: TES: $523M net CapEx for 44-hour, 180 MW system
Li-ion: $1,028M net CapEx for same system (97% more expensive)
Flow battery: $1,258M net CapEx (141% more expensive)
TES has no supply chain risk (firebrick vs. lithium/vanadium)
Trade-off: Lower efficiency (33% vs. 88%), but cost advantage dominates

priorities.name: Extend IRA 30% ITC beyond 2032
priorities.impact: Saves $337M on 100% CFE system (43% of capex)

priorities.name: Implement carbon pricing ($100+/ton CO₂)
priorities.impact: Makes 100% CFE competitive vs. gas backup

priorities.name: Create premium market for 24/7 CFE RECs

risks.risk: TES low efficiency (33%) drives up solar/wind overbuild
risks.mitigation: Optimize TES duration (44 hr optimal, not 16 or 72 hr)

risks.risk: 100% CFE has 88% LCOE premium vs. baseline ($113 vs. $60)
risks.mitigation: Phase approach: 90% CFE first ($81/MWh), then 100% as costs decline

risks.risk: Technology risk: TES at 180 MW scale unproven
risks.mitigation: Pilot 50 MW system first, validate 40% min load constraint
Deploy by 2031 to lock in 30% ITC, lobby for extension
